items.text: Notice: The Main Library will extend its hours during final exams. From May 11 to May 18, the building will remain open until 2:00 AM. Students must present a valid ID card after 11:00 PM. A $2 fine applies to overdue reserved materials. Group study rooms may be reserved online in advance.

questions.stem: According to the notice, until what time will the library stay open during finals?
questions.explanation: The notice states the building will remain open until 2:00 AM from May 11 to May 18.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who enter the library after 11:00 PM?

options.A: They receive a printed exam schedule.
options.B: They pay a nightly access charge.
options.D: They must reserve a study room.

questions.explanation: Because a valid ID card must be presented after 11:00 PM, late-night visitors need to show identification.

items.text: Attention students: The campus shuttle will follow a reduced schedule during spring break, March 9 to March 13. Buses depart from West Gate every 30 minutes between 8:00 AM and 6:00 PM. A $3 pass is required and can be purchased at the Transit Office. Riders should arrive at least five minutes early.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how often will the shuttle depart during spring break?
questions.explanation: The notice states buses depart from West Gate every 30 minutes.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who want to ride the shuttle?

options.A: They must live close to West Gate.
options.B: They must travel only in the morning.
options.C: They must speak with the driver first.
options.D: They must buy a pass beforehand.

questions.explanation: Since a $3 pass is required and sold at the Transit Office, riders must buy a pass in advance.
